title: Time to Expand the Bus Lanes to Petaling Jaya
snippet: Rapid Bus's average daily passenger volume in the Klang Valley is around 200,000. Expanding dedicated bus lanes in PJ is proposed as a critical solution to improve bus reliability and reduce peak-hour congestion.

title: PJ Targets 40% Public Transport Use by 2045
snippet: The Petaling Jaya City Council (MBPJ) has set a target of 40% public transport usage through the Petaling Jaya Integrated Plan (PIP) 2.0. The plan emphasizes better accessibility and station integration.

title: Key Issues That Shaped Petaling Jaya in 2024
snippet: Major themes for the year included infrastructure proposals and urban planning for an aging population. Residents actively raised concerns about mobility gaps and the need for more seamless urban integration.

title: Extreme Delays & Bunching: Petaling Jaya Bus Service Complaints
snippet: Commuters reported frequent wait times of 30–60 minutes for PJ City Bus and Rapid KL services, often followed by 'bus bunching' where multiple buses arrive at once.

title: High Population Density and Infrastructure Strain in PJ
snippet: As of early 2024, Petaling Jaya has a population of over 823,000 with a high density of 6,000 people per sq km, putting immense pressure on the existing transport network.

title: 'Why is traffic so bad?': PJ to Ampang Gridlock Frustrations
snippet: Residents shared frustrations over severe traffic congestion in Petaling Jaya, with some reporting commute times exceeding 2 hours for short distances (e.g., PJ to Ampang).
